title:
MO MONEY
MO PROBLEMS
subtitle: Money solves problems… until it invents new ones.
sweetSpotMessage: Congratulations. You made it to The Sweet Spot. Your wealth level has gifted you stress relief, answered most of life’s financial worries, and given you and your children stability. You are now free to use your disposable time and income to do what you truly love.
musicEmbedHtml: <iframe height="152" src="https://open.spotify.com/embed/track/4INDiWSKvqSKDEu7mh8HFz?utm_source=generator" allow="autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture" loading="lazy"></iframe>

tiers.name: Survival Mode
tiers.caption: Every month feels like a boss fight with receipts.
questions: Can I pay rent this month?
Can I cover electricity and water?
What if the car breaks down?
Can I afford groceries this week?
What if I get sick?
Can I make it to payday?
Which bill can wait?
Can I afford to see a dentist?

tiers.name: Barely Floating
tiers.caption: The basics are almost covered, but one surprise can still ruin the plot.
questions: How do I save anything?
What if my hours get cut?
Can I replace broken shoes?
Can I afford heating this winter?
What if my phone dies?
How long can I keep juggling this?
Can I help my family if they need me?

tiers.name: Bills Covered
tiers.caption: The alarms are quieter. They are still alarms.
questions: What if rent goes up again?
Can I ever get ahead?
Why does one mistake cost so much?
Can I afford a holiday once in my life?
What happens if I lose my job?
Can I raise children like this?

tiers.name: Stable Ground
tiers.caption: You can breathe now, but you still check the floor for trapdoors.
questions: Am I working just to stay stuck?
Is everyone else coping better than me?
How long can stress be normal?
Is this all life is?
How much emergency savings is enough?

tiers.name: Comfortable
tiers.caption: Life stops being only damage control.
questions: Should I invest or overpay debt?
Can I finally relax a little?
What do I actually enjoy doing?
Have I already made it more than I realize?

tiers.name: The Sweet Spot
tiers.caption: Comfortable home, passive income, future secured for children, freedom of time.

tiers.name: Extra Comfortable
tiers.caption: The first world problems begin politely.
questions: Should we go business class this time?
Is one holiday enough this year?
Do I really need a second property?
Why am I checking markets every morning?

tiers.name: Lifestyle Creep
tiers.caption: Convenience has entered its demanding era.
questions: Should I upgrade the kitchen again?
Which gym membership is best now?
Why is choosing restaurants stressful?
Do I need a better watch or a better hobby?
Why does everyone recommend a financial advisor?
Is comfort making me boring?

tiers.name: Asset Weather
tiers.caption: The portfolio has moods. You now have weather reports for your own money.
questions: Who manages all these accounts?
Why are taxes suddenly a personality trait?
Do I need another accountant?
Why does maintenance multiply by ownership?
Should I restructure everything?
Why is the second home always leaking something?

tiers.name: Admin Dynasty
tiers.caption: You escaped scarcity and entered scheduling.
questions: Are my children becoming too comfortable?
Why does someone poorer seem calmer?
How many passwords does wealth require?
Why do I have meetings about things I don’t enjoy?
Should the yacht have a sauna?
Which assistant manages the assistants?

tiers.name: Fully Detached From the Human Experience
tiers.caption: The curve has become a mirror and nobody likes that.
questions: Do I need a retreat to recover from travel?
Why do I own six calendars?
Is this enough for four generations?
Why does that billionaire still look stressed?
What exactly am I optimizing anymore?
What was the point again?
